description: Calcifer feature backlog. Only harness.py complete modifies passes. All other fields require explicit commit.

features.title: MCP auth token refresh callback
features.description: Allow long-running sessions to recover from expired MCP auth tokens via a user-provided refresh callback.
features.motivation: Sessions >30min that connect to OAuth-protected MCP servers currently break on 401. Claude Code handles this with checkAndRefreshOAuthTokenIfNeeded + handleOAuth401Error. Calcifer has no equivalent — the agent crashes mid-session.
acceptance_criteria: MCPClient accepts an optional on_auth_error callback
401/403 responses trigger the callback with the server name
Callback can return new headers (dict) or None
If new headers returned, request is retried once with them
If callback is None or returns None, original behavior (raise) preserved
New tests in tests/test_mcp.py cover the refresh + retry path
All existing mock tests still pass

features.title: Stop hooks can inject messages and continue
features.description: Allow stop hooks to return a structured result: either stop the loop, or inject messages and continue.
features.motivation: Claude Code's handleStopHooks can return blockingErrors that get injected as messages while the loop continues — used for budget warnings, content filter corrections, etc. Calcifer's stop hooks can only return bool (stop/not-stop). This limits extensibility.
acceptance_criteria: New StopHookResult dataclass with fields: stop (bool), inject_messages (list[Message])
Stop hooks can return either bool (backward compat) or StopHookResult
If inject_messages is non-empty, messages are appended to conversation before the next turn
If stop is True, loop breaks after injection (current turn still emits turn_end)
New test covers inject-and-continue path
Existing stop-hook tests (bool return) still pass

features.title: Abort signal with reason
features.description: Replace the bare asyncio.Event abort signal with a reason-aware signal distinguishing user interrupt from sibling error propagation.
features.motivation: Claude Code's AbortController carries a reason field (interrupt, sibling_error, streaming_fallback) used for debugging and synthetic error messages. Calcifer's single Event loses this distinction — tools all see the same opaque 'aborted' state.
acceptance_criteria: New AbortReason enum (user_interrupt, sibling_error, timeout, other)
Agent.abort() accepts an optional reason argument (defaults to user_interrupt)
ToolContext.abort_reason field populated when abort_signal is True
Tool result messages for aborted tools include the reason
Backward-compat: bool abort_signal still works; existing tests pass

features.title: Pre and post tool execution hooks
features.description: Add pre-tool and post-tool hook points to the execution pipeline for extensibility (logging, validation, mutation).
features.motivation: Claude Code has runPreToolUseHooks and runPostToolUseHooks run around every tool call. Enables features like audit logging, content filtering, or input rewriting without touching tool code. Calcifer currently has no extension point between validate and call.
acceptance_criteria: New HookManager.on_pre_tool and HookManager.on_post_tool registration methods
Pre-tool hooks run after validation, before tool.call()
Pre-tool hooks can veto execution (return False) or mutate input (return dict)
Post-tool hooks run after tool.call() with the ToolResult
Post-tool hooks can mutate the result (return ToolResult)
Hook failures are logged but do not crash the tool call
New tests cover pre-veto, pre-mutate, post-mutate, and hook failure paths

features.title: Skill when_to_use frontmatter field
features.description: Add when_to_use frontmatter field to skill definitions, surfaced in the skill list prompt so the LLM knows when to invoke each skill.
features.motivation: Claude Code skills have a when_to_use field that tells the model when each skill is relevant. Calcifer currently only has description, which is often too terse. Adding when_to_use improves skill selection without adding a new concept.
acceptance_criteria: SkillDefinition has a when_to_use field (str, default empty)
load_skill_file parses when-to-use from frontmatter
apply_token_budget includes when_to_use in the entry when present
Existing skills without when_to_use still load correctly
New test verifies when_to_use parsing and budget inclusion
